page: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose server instance from the Dsv7 family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores, operating at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability for external storage volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, the D192s_v7 demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Single-core performance is moderate, ranking in the middle 50% for stress-ng. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, achieving top-tier results for both small cached blocks and large uncached read/write operations. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), drives strong performance in cryptographic operations and prompt processing for large language models.

While the instance excels at parallel processing, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving, it exhibits a notable performance tradeoff in sequential text generation for LLMs, where it registers average to weak speeds. The absence of local NVMe storage requires architectural reliance on network-attached Premium Storage, which may impact workloads requiring ultra-low latency local disk access. Given its high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, the D192s_v7 is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized enterprise workloads, large-scale database hosting, Redis deployments, and intensive CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a high-capacity, general-purpose server from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs (96 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. While the instance lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior throughput for database operations and Redis. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation speeds are relatively weak. Equipped with advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), this server is highly suited for parallelized enterprise applications, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning prompt processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, ideal for large databases and parallel enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose server with 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput database and parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core, dedicated processor instance delivering exceptional multi-threaded performance and massive memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Premium Storage capable with no local disk storage
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and parallel enterprise applications
